question: Russia-Ukraine Ceasefire before GTA VI?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if  if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Only ceasefires which constitute a general pause in the conflict will qualify. Ceasefires which only apply to energy infrastructure, the Black Sea, or other similar agreements will not qualify.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution for a ceasefire will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Drake releasing his long-teased Iceman album before GTA VI's confirmed November 19, 2026 launch, pricing it at an 87% implied probability amid persistent rollout hints and no competing delays from Rockstar. GPT-6's 67% odds reflect OpenAI rumors pointing to a mid-2026 debut, outpacing music drops from Playboi Carti or Rihanna at around 54%, while geopolitical and meme outcomes hover near 50% in this $21 million volume market. Rockstar's silence since the November 2025 delay announcement—coupled with debunked save-system rumors—signals final polish on the Vice City epic, but Take-Two's May earnings call looms as a key catalyst for trailer drops or pre-orders that could tighten GTA VI timelines and reshape frontrunners.

question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
description:

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Harvey Weinstein's ultimate prison time remains tightly contested, with no prison time edging out 20-30 years at 27.4% amid his ongoing #MeToo-era legal saga. Key drivers include his upheld 2025 New York third-degree criminal sexual act conviction—yet unsentenced—plus a standing 16-year California rape term under appeal, complicated by January 2026 plea talks to avert a third Manhattan rape retrial now slated for April 14. At 73, Weinstein's deteriorating health fuels release speculation via compassionate terms or credits, but conviction risks could stack decades concurrently. Traders eye the retrial verdict and appeals as pivotal, underscoring persistent uncertainty in this landmark Hollywood accountability case.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Colorado Avalanche lead Polymarket's 2026 Stanley Cup trader consensus at 20.1% implied probability, fueled by their dominant 50-16-10 regular-season record and 110 points atop the Central Division, with Nathan MacKinnon's league-leading 51 goals driving offense amid a late 1-2-0 week. Tampa Bay Lightning (14.3%) and Carolina Hurricanes (12.5%) trail closely in the bunched top tier, reflecting Eastern Conference parity—Lightning's 48-23-6 mark and Nikita Kucherov's 126 points contrasting Hurricanes' 49-22-6 surge (3-1-0 last week)—while Dallas Stars (7.8%) and Edmonton Oilers (7.5%) benefit from solid Central/Pacific positioning. Playoffs looming April 18 keep the race tight, with wild-card battles, home-ice advantages, and goaltending edges like Andrei Vasilevskiy's wins shaping crowd wisdom on deep playoff runs.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's 40.5% implied probability atop the 2026 NBA Champion market stems from their league-best 62-16 record, clinched No. 1 Western Conference seed, and status as defending champions after topping the Pacers in 2025, bolstered by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play despite a recent abdominal strain recovery. San Antonio Spurs at 15.3% surged on an 11-game win streak to 60-19 and the No. 2 West seed, fueled by Victor Wembanyama's dominance. Boston Celtics' 11.6% reflects their strong No. 2 East position at 53-25 amid playoff clinchings, while Denver Nuggets' 10.5% holds via Nikola Jokić's triple-double averages despite a No. 4 West tie. Detroit Pistons lead East at 57-22 but trail at 5.7% due to perceived roster limits versus Western depth, with recent win streaks and top-6 seeds shaping trader consensus as playoffs near April 18.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's 40.5% implied probability atop the 2026 NBA Champion market stems from their league-best 62-16 record, clinched No. 1 Western Conference seed, and status as defending champions after topping the Pacers in 2025, bolstered by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play despite a recent abdominal strain recovery. San Antonio Spurs at 15.3% surged on an 11-game win streak to 60-19 and the No. 2 West seed, fueled by Victor Wembanyama's dominance. Boston Celtics' 11.6% reflects their strong No. 2 East position at 53-25 amid playoff clinchings, while Denver Nuggets' 10.5% holds via Nikola Jokić's triple-double averages despite a No. 4 West tie. Detroit Pistons lead East at 57-22 but trail at 5.7% due to perceived roster limits versus Western depth, with recent win streaks and top-6 seeds shaping trader consensus as playoffs near April 18.

question: Will the Atlanta Hawks win the 2026 NBA Finals?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Atlanta Hawks win the 2026 NBA Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

question: MegaETH market cap (FDV) >$2B one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of MegaETH's token is greater than $2,000,000,000 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 24 hours after launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If MegaETH doesn't launch a token by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No."

question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain leads trader consensus at 16% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup following the recent group draw and April 1 FIFA rankings placing France atop ahead of Spain and defending champions Argentina, yet the top contenders remain tightly bunched amid the expanded 48-team format where group stage top-two plus best thirds advance to the round of 32. Spain benefits from a favorable Group H matchup against Uruguay, Saudi Arabia, and Cape Verde, bolstering their Euro 2024 momentum and young stars like Yamal; France faces Senegal, Norway, and Iraq in Group I; England draws Croatia, Ghana, and Panama in Group L; while Argentina eyes Algeria, Austria, and Jordan in Group J. March play-offs finalized the field with surprises like Italy's elimination, underscoring tournament depth and upset potential just two months from kickoff on June 11.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain leads trader consensus at 16% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup following the recent group draw and April 1 FIFA rankings placing France atop ahead of Spain and defending champions Argentina, yet the top contenders remain tightly bunched amid the expanded 48-team format where group stage top-two plus best thirds advance to the round of 32. Spain benefits from a favorable Group H matchup against Uruguay, Saudi Arabia, and Cape Verde, bolstering their Euro 2024 momentum and young stars like Yamal; France faces Senegal, Norway, and Iraq in Group I; England draws Croatia, Ghana, and Panama in Group L; while Argentina eyes Algeria, Austria, and Jordan in Group J. March play-offs finalized the field with surprises like Italy's elimination, underscoring tournament depth and upset potential just two months from kickoff on June 11.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cooper Flagg's explosive late-season surge, including a historic 51-point outburst against Orlando followed by 45 points versus the Lakers—marking back-to-back 40-plus games as the first rookie since Allen Iverson—has propelled him to a 68% implied probability as trader consensus favorite for 2025-26 NBA Rookie of the Year. With four games left, Kia Rookie Ladder leaders Flagg and Knueppel dominate sentiment amid negligible chances for others like Dylan Harper or Ace Bailey due to lesser production and minutes.
